app_topics: the material, component and element composition of products, asked two ways: the query-optimized fq: dataset, and the baseline composition futuram:CompositionStatement dataset
example_questions: How much copper is in elvBEV?
How much copper is in the car?
Which elements does the motor contain, and in what amounts?
What is the iron fraction of low carbon steel?

endpoints.label: FutuRaM fq (query-optimized dataset)
endpoints.description: The QUERY-OPTIMIZED fq: dataset over CLASSES. One pattern answers everything: ?class fq:contains [ fq:constituent ?x ; fq:amount ?frac ]. Amounts are already aggregated kg/kg fractions; declare the constituent KIND with rdfs:subClassOf futuram:Element/Material/Component. No tree traversal, no unit normalisation, no manual aggregation.

endpoints.label: FutuRaM composition (baseline composition dataset)
endpoints.description: The BASELINE COMPOSITION futuram:CompositionStatement dataset on INSTANCES. Amounts live in the statement tree: ?whole futuram:hasCompositionStatement ?stmt . ?stmt futuram:hasPartRelation ?pr . ?pr futuram:refersTo ?partInstance ; futuram:hasQuantity/futuram:hasBestValue [ qudt:numericValue ?v ; qudt:unit ?u ]. Units are MIXED (kg/kg vs g/kg). There is NO precomputed aggregate: a constituent's share of a whole several hops up must be obtained by multiplying fractions down each whole->part path, normalising units, and summing paths. This is what the fq: view precomputes.
